titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Tom Morin Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Arts Library Special Collections Staff

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10123106.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday September 1, 2020 at 23:00

did.abstract: The collection was created by Tom Morin in the process of writing the book Threads of Influence: The Visual History of a Life in Graphic Design .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tom Morin Papers, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Threads of Influence: The Visual History of a Life in Graphic Design [Haas Arts Library, Call # NJ18.M82258 A12 2011 (LC)].

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tom Morin (MFA '68) is a graphic designer who worked at the Xerox Design Center and Westinghouse before becoming a principal at several important design firms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection was created by Tom Morin in the process of writing the book Threads of Influence: The Visual History of a Life in Graphic Design . In addition to examples of Morin's own work, the collection also includes design specimens collected by Morin to represent the many designers and firms he considers to be influences.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Biographical, 1908-2012. II. Clients, 1966-2006. III. Designers and Firms, 1938-1986.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains biographical materials about the Morin family, including Tom's father, Thomas O. Morin, as well as materials from Tom's childhood and education.

did.unittitle: Morin Brothers stock certificate with pencil
Syracuse University diploma and commencement book
Graphic College of Denmark photos and ephemera
Drawings and letter on stationery designed by Morin, from Denmark
"Introduction to Proportions..." paper and letter designs by Morin
"Laws of Organization in Perceptual Forms" by Max Wertheimer
"Italic Type: Its History, Form and Use" (Morin thesis project)
Mixtec Manuscript Style: Pre-conquest, Codex Fejervary-Mayer by Thomas D. Morin
"Italic Type" poster (modern reprint of cover to thesis book)
Yale letter of admission, diploma, awards, etc.
Typographic print with Morin quote signed by T. Vitale and numbered 1/10
Exhibition card for "Tom Morin: Thread of Influence: The Visual History of a Life in Graphic Design "
Artscope , January/February 2012 issue with article about Tom Morin exhibition at Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tom Morin has been affiliated with various design firms since receiving his MFA from Yale in 1968. This series contains examples of graphic design work produced throughout this career, from a summer internship at the Xerox Corporate Design Center to his more recent projects with Context Design, Inc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged chronologically by design firm.

did.unittitle: Parks Morin Hall Brennan and Sattelberg Architects
Burns International Security Services Annual Report
Champion Papers, "Man and His Machines: Champion Papers Courtland Mill Expansion"
Xerox, "Ten Thousand Years of Recorded Information"
New York Design , Volume One. Cincinnati, OH: Hennegan Company, 1986.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the first volume in a series documenting New York area graphic designers. Jack Hough Associates, Inc. is one of the firms featured, and Tom Morin is pictured in a group portrait (see p. 19).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two copies of "Industrial Construction from the Ground Up" for Stimm Associates, Inc. (undated) and one copy of an "Concourse/74 Agenda: Industry Equipment and Services Marketing Forum." This work may or may not date from Morin's tenure at Jack Hough Associates, Inc.

did.unittitle: Becton Dickinson, "1897-1997: Celebrating the First One Hundred"
Katonah Museum "In Line with Al Hirschfeld"
The Canyon Poem by Judy Tuwaletstiwa. Galisteo, NM: The Galisteo Press, 1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Design by Tom Morin. One copy is enclosed in a slipcase.

did.unittitle: Center for Contemporary Arts, Santa Fe
Museum of Fine Arts, Santa Fe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains examples of works collected by Tom Morin to represent the many designers or firms he considers to be influences. Among those represented are teachers Morin studied with while at Yale.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by the name of the designer or design firm.

did.unittitle: Fortune , May & June 1944 [Peter Piening, Art Director & cover design]
Fortune , July & August 1944 [Peter Piening, Art Director & cover design]
Fortune , September & October 1944 [Peter Piening, Art Director & cover design]
Fortune , March & September 1945 [Peter Piening, cover design]
Architectural Record , November 1949 [Peter Piening, cover design]
Architectural Record , January & July 1951 [Peter Piening, cover design and July design consultant]
Architectural Record , February 1952 [Peter Piening, cover design and design consultant]
Cummins Engine Company Graphics Identification Project
The Graphic Art of Paul Rand [exhibition pamphlet] and Guidelines for Using IBM Trademarks
